title: Maybe this is where Anno came from
display_title: Maybe this is where Anno came from
wikitext:
[]: Maybe this is where Anno came from...             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Aug 11, 2010 11:46 pm Apperantly, there's an asteroid named after Anno. Accoring to legend, only a piece of this asteroid, his original home, can make him lose his incredible writing powers.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Aug 12, 2010 12:09 am Then it must have hit him right between the eyes just before he sat down to start making rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Thu Aug 12, 2010 12:21 am To gift him with such a great idea.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Aug 12, 2010 1:37 am Duke wrote: To gift him with such a great idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Thu Aug 12, 2010 1:43 am generally

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Thu Aug 12, 2010 2:49 am That asteroid contains the real life Lilith, giving Anno the Fruit of Wisdom inside his own head. That explains everything! And Gerard Butler has the Fruit of Power.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenithfleet » Thu Aug 12, 2010 7:25 am Gamer137 wrote: That asteroid contains the real life Lilith, giving Anno the Fruit of Wisdom inside his own head. That explains everything! And Gerard Butler has the Fruit of Power.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Aug 12, 2010 9:19 am Folks, this is the ultimate piece of Gainax merchandise. Start working on your personal space programs now.

[]: I'm pudding my bet on a time warp. Mmm... pudding.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Aug 12, 2010 10:34 am It says it was discovered in '94, though, which was before Eva. Why would someone want to name it after Anno before he became Japan's biggest troll?

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Thu Aug 12, 2010 10:53 am Killer Bee, it has an alternate name listed in the information panel on the right. Wikipedia wrote: Alternate name(s) ... 1994 VY

[]: Time warps are like women: Never there when you need them.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Aug 12, 2010 10:59 am Duke wrote: Killer Bee, it has an alternate name listed in the information panel on the right. going by this, it was probably (re)named after Anno sometime after it was originally discovered.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Aug 12, 2010 11:48 am all kidding aside, i have to say... this is freakin' awesome . otaku WIN. crap, i'll have to rethink my list of names for celestial bodies i discover, now. seems like "Asuka" has been bumped a spot.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Thu Aug 12, 2010 11:53 am What about if we had an evageeks comic named after the site?

[]: Re: I'm pudding my bet on a time warp. Mmm... pudding.             Quote Post by Oz » Thu Aug 12, 2010 11:57 am Killer Bee wrote: It says it was discovered in '94, though, which was before Eva. Why would someone want to name it after Anno before he became Japan's biggest troll?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 12, 2010 10:31 pm This Thread = WIN

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Aug 12, 2010 10:37 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: This Thread = WIN

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion Unit-187 » Thu Aug 19, 2010 7:17 pm child of Lilith wrote: Then it must have hit him right between the eyes just before he sat down to start making rebuild.
summary:
[]: Maybe this is where Anno came from...             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Aug 11, 2010 11:46 pm Apperantly, there's an asteroid named after Anno. Accoring to legend, only a piece of this asteroid, his original home, can make him lose his incredible writing powers.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Aug 12, 2010 12:09 am Then it must have hit him right betwee
